summary.intent: Implement a framework-agnostic DatePicker state machine in crates/ars-components/src/date_time/date_picker/ following a spec (§1.7), using TDD with proptest, achieving high test coverage and passing CI.
summary.outcome: PR #697 was created and pushed across 4+ commits with a fully implemented DatePicker state machine at ~100% function / 99.85% line coverage, along with fixes to xtask lint category matching bugs, a public date_order() API, and several spec-driven corrections discovered during 4 rounds of Codex review.

repo: CalendarDate fields are pub(crate); use accessors year()->i32, month()->u8, day()->u8; new_gregorian returns Result; no Ord impl — use .compare() instead
Machine trait requires 'type Effect: EffectName'; effects use PendingEffect::named(), not use_platform_effects() in core
ars-i18n had a private date_order(locale) with full CLDR/Intl/fallback logic — DateField and DatePicker were both reimplementing only the fallback heuristic; making it public eliminated duplication
CalendarDate::to_iso8601() exists and should be used for hidden date input value serialization, not manual year()/month()/day() concatenation
Service::new takes refs not values; send is #[must_use] requiring drop(svc.send(...))
is_date_unavailable prop must be Option<calendar::IsDateUnavailableFn> (Callback type), not a plain fn pointer
MessageFn is PartialEq via Arc pointer equality; Context/Messages derive Clone + manual Debug but not PartialEq
sync_props_into_ctx must only re-sync input_text when value/format changed, not on every prop sync, to avoid erasing in-progress typing
requested_value and requested_open context fields are needed for adapter notification in controlled mode
Controlled open must BLOCK state mutation when the open prop is controlled — differs from popover-parity commit model, and this was revised mid-PR
nzu8 helper referenced in the spec does not exist anywhere in the codebase — the spec fabricated it
xtask spec/compile_snippets.rs only does syn syntax parsing, not type/borrow checking; rust,no_check opt-out is available
FocusIn-open path must NOT emit FocusCalendar — focus stays in the input for typing; only trigger/ArrowDown move focus into the grid
Snapshot CI check runs on push before the snapshot-reviewed label is added; ci.yml pull_request trigger has no 'labeled' event type, requiring a manual job re-run

code.finding: is_component_category matched hyphenated names (date-time) but dirs and snapshot paths use underscores (date_time), silently skipping entire date-time and data-display categories from the snapshot-count gate
is_ars_components_component had the same hyphen-vs-underscore bug affecting the floor calculation for snapshot counts, causing rating_group to get the wrong (too-high) floor
Private date_order(locale) function with full CLDR/Intl/fallback logic existed but was private; DateField and DatePicker were both reimplementing only the fallback heuristic
CalendarDate::to_iso8601() returns YYYY-MM-DD and is the canonical way to serialize a date for hidden inputs

workflow: TDD approach: write tests first, watch them fail, then implement — proptest caught a real controlled/uncontrolled flip bug where open Bindable diverged from State
llvm-cov used to identify uncovered branches and drive toward 100% function / 99.85% line coverage
cargo insta workflow: run insta test to create pending snapshots, review, cargo insta accept, then run --unreferenced=reject as a separate gate
4 rounds of Codex review were needed, each surfacing second-order issues from prior fixes — iterative review is essential for state machine correctness and controlled-state semantics
Fixing infrastructure bugs (xtask lint gate) can reveal pre-existing failures (rating_group snapshot count) that must be bundled into the same PR
Spec had ~10 compile-blockers vs real codebase; always validate spec types and helpers against actual codebase before implementing

friction: Spec §1.7 had ~10 compile-blockers: fabricated nzu8 helper, wrong CalendarDate API (no Ord, fields not pub, new_gregorian returns Result), wrong effect registration pattern, missing type Effect, wrong is_date_unavailable type
xtask lint silently skipped entire date-time and data-display categories due to hyphen-vs-underscore mismatch — failures were invisible until the bug was found and fixed mid-PR
rating_group had a pre-existing snapshot count deficit (8 vs floor of 9) that only surfaced after fixing the lint gate, requiring an unexpected bundled fix
Snapshot CI check timing issue: runs on push before the snapshot-reviewed label is added, requiring a manual job re-run with no automated trigger
xci-fast failed on first run due to a pre-existing staged pagination/mod.rs nightly-fmt issue unrelated to the PR
Pre-existing utility/highlight snapshot orphans block --unreferenced=reject globally
sync_props_into_ctx re-syncing input_text unconditionally erased in-progress typing — subtle state management bug requiring targeted fix after Codex review
open Bindable was a redundant write-only mirror that could diverge from State in controlled/uncontrolled flip — proptest caught this, required removing the Bindable entirely
open_items: Pre-existing utility/highlight snapshot orphans still block bare cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject — needs cleanup in a separate PR
94 pre-existing crate-wide cargo doc warnings remain unaddressed
xtask spec/compile_snippets.rs only does syn parse — spec code snippets can reference non-existent helpers without being caught; full type-check would require a separate effort

ci.yml pull_request trigger has no 'labeled' event type — snapshot-reviewed PRs always need a manual job re-run; options are adding 'labeled' trigger (re-runs full CI expensively) or switching to CHANGELOG-entry-based acknowledgement
Full cargo xci (not just xci-fast) recommended before merge due to the i18n foundation change (date_order) touching the wasm web-intl path validated only by nightly CI
Codex pass 4 was still in progress when the transcript ended — PR may still have unresolved review threads
